mnemonics: B.A.G. - Bootstrap AGGregating: Bootstrapping samples and AGGregating their predictions to reduce variance and improve stability.
Bag of Learners: Imagine a bag (bootstrap sample) full of base learners (models) whose individual predictions are combined (averaged) for a more robust final answer.
keyPoints: Bagging reduces the variance of a single model by training multiple models on different bootstrap samples of the data.
The final prediction is made by averaging (for regression) or taking a majority vote (for classification) of all the individual model predictions.
Bagging is particularly effective for high-variance, low-bias models (like deep decision trees) and is the foundation for the powerful Random Forest algorithm.
commonMistakes: Confusing Bagging (parallel training on different data) with Boosting (sequential training on weighted data).
Assuming bagging reduces bias; its primary benefit is reducing variance and overfitting, not necessarily improving bias.
Forgetting that the base models in bagging are typically of the same type (homogeneous), e.g., all decision trees.
